questions.title: Start a Node.js Application on Port 3000
questions.task: Create a server that reads PORT from the environment, falls back to 3000, and logs the listening URL after startup.
questions.starterCode:
const PORT = Number(process.env.PORT) || 3000;

// TODO: create the server and listen on PORT
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Use process.env.PORT and provide a numeric fallback.
sourceQuestions: How to start a nodejs application in port 3000 (practical)

questions.title: Create and Use a Custom Node.js Module
questions.task: Create a local module that exports an add function and import it from another file.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Use named ESM exports or module.exports/require consistently.

questions.title: Install and Use a Development Dependency
questions.task: Initialize a package and install nodemon as a devDependency, then add a dev script that starts the application with nodemon.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Use npm install -D nodemon and set the dev script to nodemon app.js.

questions.title: Read and Validate Environment Variables
questions.task: Read configuration from process.env, convert PORT to a number, and throw a clear startup error when a required value is missing.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Environment variables are strings; validate and convert them at startup.
sourceQuestions: How to access a value in env file,

questions.title: Set Environment Variables Without dotenv
questions.task: Demonstrate how to set and read an environment variable using the operating-system shell and process.env.
questions.starterCode:
const value = process.env.APP_MODE;

// TODO: validate and print APP_MODE
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Use platform-appropriate shell syntax or cross-env for portable npm scripts.
sourceQuestions: how to set an environment value
how to create an env variable

questions.title: Create a File If Missing, Otherwise Read It
questions.task: If a file does not exist, create it with default content. If it exists, read and return its content.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Attempt readFile() and handle ENOENT by calling writeFile().
sourceQuestions: API endpoint to create a file if not exists else read it

questions.title: Create a File with fs/promises
questions.task: Create a UTF-8 text file asynchronously and avoid overwriting it when it already exists.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Use the wx flag and handle EEXIST.
sourceQuestions: How to create a file using fs

questions.title: Create a GET Endpoint That Reads a File
questions.task: Create GET /file that asynchronously reads a UTF-8 file and sends its content. Return 404 when the file is missing.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Use async/await and distinguish ENOENT from unexpected errors.
sourceQuestions: create http get endpoint to read content from a file

questions.title: Create a Hard Link with fs.link()
questions.task: Create a hard link from an existing source file to a new destination path and handle an existing destination cleanly.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Use fs/promises link(source, destination).

questions.title: Delete a File with the FS Module
questions.task: Delete a file asynchronously. Return a useful result when the file does not exist.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Catch ENOENT separately from unexpected errors.

questions.title: Practice Core File-System Operations
questions.task: Implement functions to create, read, update, rename, inspect, and delete a UTF-8 file using fs/promises.
questions.starterCode:
import * as fs from "node:fs/promises";

// TODO: implement create, read, update, rename, stat, and remove functions
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Use writeFile, readFile, appendFile, rename, stat, and unlink.

questions.task: Rename a file asynchronously from an old path to a new path.

Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.

questions.title: Write Content to a File

Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.

questions.title: Parse a URL with the WHATWG URL API
questions.task: Parse a URL and return its protocol, hostname, port, pathname, and query parameters.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Use new URL() and Object.fromEntries(url.searchParams).

questions.title: Create a Basic Node.js HTTP Server
questions.task: Create an HTTP server without Express. Return a JSON response for GET / and return 404 for unknown routes.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Check req.method and req.url, set Content-Type, and finish with res.end().
sourceQuestions: Create a basic server using nodejs - P
Create a server using Node js
Create a server using HTTP - P

questions.title: Send Data with an HTTP GET Request
questions.task: Create a Node.js client request that sends values as URL query parameters and parses the JSON response.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Build the URL with URL and URLSearchParams; GET requests normally carry data in the URL.
sourceQuestions: How to pass data in GET method
how to send data in http.get()

questions.title: Create an HTTP GET Endpoint
questions.task: Handle GET /health with the Node.js http module and return a JSON health response. Return 404 for other routes.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Check both req.method and req.url.

questions.title: Set HTTP Status and Headers with writeHead()
questions.task: Create a response that uses writeHead() to send status 201 and a JSON Content-Type header, then end the response with JSON data.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Call writeHead() before res.end().

questions.title: Create a Division API Endpoint
questions.task: Create GET /divide?a=NUMBER&b=NUMBER. Return the quotient, reject invalid numbers, and reject division by zero.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Validate both values before performing division.
sourceQuestions: api end point to divide 2 numbers,

questions.title: Create an API to Add Two Numbers
questions.task: Create POST /sum that accepts JSON with a and b, validates both numbers, and returns their sum.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Read req.body after express.json() and validate with Number.isFinite().

questions.title: Fetch JSON from a REST API in Node.js
questions.task: Call a REST endpoint with fetch(), verify response.ok, parse JSON, and handle network or HTTP errors.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Check response.ok before calling response.json().
sourceQuestions: read data from REST API and print it as JS object

questions.title: Create a Basic Express Server
questions.task: Create an Express server with GET / that returns a JSON message and listen on port 3000.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Create app with express(), register app.get(), then call app.listen().

questions.title: Add Two Numbers with Path and Query Parameters
questions.task: Create endpoints that add two numbers: one using path parameters and one using query parameters. Validate numeric input.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Convert strings with Number() and reject Number.isNaN() values.

questions.title: Find a User with a Route Parameter
questions.task: Create GET /users/:userName. Search a predefined username array and return the user when found; otherwise return 404.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Read req.params.userName and use Array.includes().
sourceQuestions: Create a route with a path parameter userName and check whether it exists in a predefined array.

questions.title: Read a URL Path Parameter
questions.task: Create GET /products/:id and return the id from req.params. Reject an empty or invalid numeric id.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Read req.params.id and convert it to a number when required.
sourceQuestions: Pass a value as url parameter in api

questions.title: Read Multiple Query Parameters
questions.task: Create GET /search that reads q, page, and limit from req.query, applies defaults, and returns the normalized values.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Query values are strings; convert and validate numeric fields.
sourceQuestions: how to pass multiple queries in url

questions.title: Read User Input in a POST Route
questions.task: Configure JSON and URL-encoded body parsing, then create POST /users that reads and validates name and email from req.body.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Body-parsing middleware must be registered before the route.
sourceQuestions: how to access userinput values in post method

questions.title: Serve Static Files with Express
questions.task: Serve files from a public directory and make index.html available from the root URL.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Pass an absolute directory path to express.static().
sourceQuestions: static file serving in nodejs - Practical

questions.title: Add Middleware to an API Route
questions.task: Create reusable middleware that adds a request ID to req and include that ID in the API response.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Set req.requestId and call next().

questions.title: Log Incoming Request Parameters
questions.task: Create middleware that logs route parameters, query parameters, and body keys for every incoming request without logging sensitive values.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Log body field names rather than secrets such as passwords or tokens.
sourceQuestions: middleware to log names of every incoming parameters,

questions.title: Middleware to Block All Incoming Requests
questions.task: Create middleware that immediately returns a maintenance response for every incoming request.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Send the response without calling next().
sourceQuestions: block all incoming request to server using middle ware

questions.title: Middleware to Block DELETE Requests
questions.task: Create middleware that rejects DELETE requests with status 403 and passes all other requests to the next handler.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Use req.method and return immediately after sending the response.
sourceQuestions: middleware to block all DELETE methods

questions.title: Middleware to Block POST Requests
questions.task: Create application middleware that rejects every POST request with status 403 and allows other methods to continue.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Compare req.method and call next() only for allowed methods.
sourceQuestions: middleware to block all POST requests,
middleware to block all POST method requests

questions.title: Create a Router Endpoint with Router-Level Middleware
questions.task: Create an Express Router, attach router-level logging middleware, and add GET /profile that returns JSON.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Call router.use() before defining router.get().
sourceQuestions: Practical: write an http endpoint and add a middleware in router level

questions.title: Create Application-Level Middleware
questions.task: Create application-level middleware that logs the request method, path, and current time, then calls next().
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Read req.method and req.originalUrl, then call next().
sourceQuestions: application level middleware to block GET Request and record other incoming request to a file (PUT - current time) -P
application level middleware to block GET Request and -record other incoming request to a file (PUT - current time)

questions.title: Create Router-Level Middleware
questions.task: Create an Express Router with middleware that runs only for routes registered on that router.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Register router.use() before the router routes.
sourceQuestions: how to setup router level middleware
how to setup a router level middleware
How to use router level middleware

questions.title: Implement Custom Express Middleware
questions.task: Implement request logging, header validation, and response-time middleware and demonstrate their registration order.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Every non-terminal middleware must call next() exactly once.

questions.title: Validate a Required Request Header
questions.task: Create middleware that requires an x-api-key header. Return 401 when it is missing or invalid; otherwise call next().
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Use req.get() or req.headers and compare against configuration.
sourceQuestions: middle ware to validate a particular header

questions.title: Validate Express Form Input
questions.task: Validate required fields in req.body and return 400 with field-level messages when validation fails.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Normalize input and keep validation middleware before the controller.

questions.title: Configure CORS in Express
questions.task: Configure CORS to allow a specified frontend origin and the GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, and OPTIONS methods.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Use the cors package with origin and methods options.
sourceQuestions: How to configure CORS in nodejs
How to set up cors policy

questions.title: Handle an OPTIONS Preflight Request
questions.task: Handle OPTIONS requests by returning the required CORS headers and a 204 response without a response body.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Set allow-origin, allow-methods, and allow-headers before ending the response.

questions.title: Create a Protected Express Route
questions.task: Create authentication middleware that verifies a bearer token and allows access to a protected route only when verification succeeds.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Extract the Authorization header, verify the token, attach the user to req, then call next().

questions.title: Create and Verify a JWT
questions.task: Create a signed JWT containing a user ID and role, then verify it and return the decoded payload. Reject invalid or expired tokens.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Use jwt.sign() and jwt.verify(); never hardcode the production secret.
sourceQuestions: Create JWT token and verify token

questions.title: Initialize and Use Express Sessions
questions.task: Configure express-session, store a user identifier after login, and read it from a protected route.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Configure secret, resave, saveUninitialized, and cookie options.

questions.title: Set and Expire an HTTP Cookie
questions.task: Set an HTTP-only cookie with SameSite and Max-Age options, and provide a route that clears it.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Use res.cookie() and res.clearCookie() with matching options.
sourceQuestions: How to set expiry for a cookies

questions.title: Convert Callback Hell to Promises
questions.task: Take a sequence of nested asynchronous callbacks and rewrite it using Promises, then consume it with async/await.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Wrap callback APIs in Promise constructors or use util.promisify().
sourceQuestions: Create a callback hell and convert the code into promise

questions.title: Offload a CPU-Heavy Task to a Worker Thread
questions.task: Run a CPU-heavy calculation in a worker thread and send the result back to the main thread without blocking the server.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Pass input with workerData and respond with parentPort.postMessage().

questions.title: Run a Command with a Child Process
questions.task: Start a child process using spawn, capture stdout and stderr, and resolve with the exit code.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Listen for data, error, and close events.
sourceQuestions: How to create a child process

questions.title: Read a Large File with a Stream
questions.task: Read a large UTF-8 file using createReadStream and process it chunk by chunk.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Listen for data, end, and error events, or use for await...of.

questions.title: Transfer Data Between Files with pipe()
questions.task: Copy data from a source file to a destination file using readable.pipe(writable) or pipeline().
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: pipeline() forwards errors and resolves when copying is complete.

questions.title: Implement a Simple EventEmitter Flow
questions.task: Create an EventEmitter, subscribe to a custom event, emit it with data, and demonstrate the difference between on() and once().
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Emit the same event twice after registering both on() and once().

questions.title: Configure EJS and Render Dynamic Data
questions.task: Configure EJS as the view engine and render a homepage that receives a username or email from the route handler.

Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Use app.set("view engine", "ejs") and res.render(view, data).

questions.title: Create Express Error-Handling Middleware
questions.task: Create four-argument Express error middleware that returns a consistent JSON error response.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: The signature must be (err, req, res, next), and the middleware should be registered last.

how to setup an error handling middleware

questions.title: Encrypt and Decrypt a String
questions.task: Encrypt a UTF-8 string with Node.js crypto using an authenticated cipher and decrypt it back to the original value.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Use AES-GCM with a random IV and preserve the authentication tag.

questions.title: Hash and Verify a Password with bcrypt
questions.task: Hash a password with bcrypt and verify a login candidate with bcrypt.compare(). Never decrypt or store the plaintext password.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
questions.hint: Use a reasonable salt-round value and await both operations.
